scout_patches.reason: Injury landscape shows Anthony Davis and D'Angelo Russell both OUT for the Lakers, which directly triggers franchise player rules and needs to be embedded as current context to prevent erroneous Lakers picks.

The standings reveal a stark stratification: OKC (52-15) and the new-look Detroit Pistons / San Antonio Spurs (both 48-18) are elite, while Washington (L10: 0-10), Brooklyn, Utah, and Sacramento are deep in tank territory — these matchups should provide consistent edge opportunities. The Lakers are effectively compromised with both Davis and Russell out despite a strong overall record, making them a team to fade rather than back until roster health improves.

notes: No bets have been settled yet so there is no performance signal to act on. The most notable macro trends this session are: (1) Washington Wizards hitting 0-10 in their last 10 is an extreme tank signal — fade them aggressively when line allows; (2) Dallas Mavericks are now a confirmed tank team at 1-9 L10; (3) Atlanta Hawks (W8 streak, 9-1 L10) and Detroit Pistons (48-18) are surprise contenders worth tracking for motivational overlays as they fight for seeding. Lakers resilience despite dual absences (41-25, W4) suggests books may already be pricing in the injuries — avoid over-applying the franchise-player penalty in non-ML markets.
